what_it_does: Reads and answers questions about the self-describing service registry (services.json): loading, querying services/groups/combos, computing approximate VRAM and RAM costs, resolving startup targets, detecting shared ports, and safely editing per-service config fields.
observations: stdlib-only and owns no runtime state
Derives REG_PATH from the module location (.../ops/services.json)
Provides both vram_of and ram_of cost accessors, treating 0 as 'no resident footprint'
combos() implements a variant inheritance mechanism via extends/swap/remove/add with fail-loud validation
resolve() handles default (autostart), all, @combo, group, and single service targets
shared_ports() identifies ports claimed by multiple services to avoid false-positive running checks
set_config() requires an existing config block and coerces string values to bool/int/float before saving
standouts: Combo variant resolver with extends/swap/remove/add and registry-is-truth validation
Dual VRAM/RAM cost model distinguishing GPU and host memory footprints
shared_ports guard against sibling port collisions
conventions: Fail-loud exceptions for missing combos, unknown services, swap mismatches, and extends cycles
Pretty JSON serialization with trailing newline
Local import of collections.Counter inside shared_ports
concerns: load() and save() are hard-wired to the global REG_PATH; not parameterizable for tests
set_config() rewrites the entire registry file for a single field change
resolve() assumes group names do not collide with service keys (group checked before service)
notes: This is the read/query brain for services.json, with a small safe write path for per-service config tweaks.
questions: What is the exact JSON schema of services.json (required vs optional fields)?
Who consumes the combo/resolve outputs and in what startup order?
Is the default vram_ceiling_mb of 16376 tied to a specific host GPU?
What does the migration path in cli/UPDATING.md look like for legacy script services?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the module loads and queries the registry without owning state, and provides the config edit path it claims to own.
apparent_themes: Registry as single source of truth
Resource budgeting across GPU and host RAM
intention_signals: To centralize all knowledge of the service registry in one small, dependency-free module so CLI commands can ask declarative questions about what should run and what it costs.
novelty: The combo variant inheritance (extends/swap/add/remove) embedded directly in the service registry resolver.
contribution: A stateless, stdlib-only registry access layer that exposes service metadata, resource-cost accessors, combo-variant resolution, target expansion, shared-port detection, and a guarded config-edit helper.
summary_for_embedding: company/ops/cli/registry.py is a stdlib-only module that loads services.json, exposes service/group/combo queries, computes VRAM and RAM footprints, resolves startup targets, detects shared ports, and provides a guarded per-service config editor.
intention_for_embedding: To give CLI tooling a single, dependency-free source of truth for discovering services, their resource costs, loadout variants, and editable configuration fields.

symbols.does: Opens and parses services.json into a Python object.
symbols.description: load() reads the registry file at REG_PATH and returns its parsed JSON contents; it is the primary entry point for registry data.

symbols.does: Returns the services mapping from a loaded registry.
symbols.description: services(reg) extracts reg['services'], the dictionary of service-key to service-metadata.

symbols.does: Returns the groups mapping from a loaded registry.
symbols.description: groups(reg) extracts reg['groups'], the dictionary mapping group names to their member service keys.

symbols.does: Returns the enforced VRAM budget in megabytes.
symbols.description: ceiling_mb(reg) reads the optional vram_ceiling_mb field, defaulting to 16376 MB, which the resource manager uses as the GPU memory cap.

symbols.does: Returns the approximate GPU VRAM footprint of a service in MB.
symbols.description: vram_of(svc) looks for a top-level vram_mb field, then load.vram_mb, and falls back to 0 to mean the service does not occupy the GPU.

symbols.does: Returns the absolute path to a service's serve script, if any.
symbols.description: serve_script(svc) expands the optional serve field through os.path.expanduser, returning None when no serve script is configured.

symbols.does: Resolves named service loadouts, including variant inheritance.
symbols.description: combos(reg) returns all combos except the _doc key, resolving extends/swap/remove/add inheritance into concrete service lists and validating every service against the registry; it raises on missing bases, unknown services, bad swaps, or extends cycles.

symbols.does: Pretty-prints the registry back to services.json.
symbols.description: save(reg) serializes the registry dictionary to REG_PATH as indented JSON with a trailing newline, used after config edits or swaps.

symbols.does: Converts a string value to bool, int, or float when possible.
symbols.description: _coerce(s) leaves non-string values unchanged, parses 'true'/'false' to booleans, then tries int and float, returning the original string if no conversion applies.

symbols.does: Sets one field in a service's existing config block and persists the registry.
symbols.description: set_config(reg, key, field, value) requires the service to already have a config block, coerces the new value, writes it into the block, saves the registry, and returns the coerced value.

symbols.does: Finds ports used by more than one service.
symbols.description: shared_ports(reg) counts occurrences of the port field across services and returns the set of ports that appear more than once, so callers know port-open checks may be ambiguous.

symbols.does: Expands a startup target into a list of service keys.
symbols.description: resolve(reg, target) converts None/'default' to autostart services, 'all' to every service, '@name' to a resolved combo, a group name to its members, or a service key to a single-element list, raising KeyError for unknown targets.
